title: Zephyranthes ischihualasta
taxonomy: Zephyranthes ischihualasta is classified within the family Amaryllidaceae and is a species of flowering plant, specifically a type of zephyranth. More specifically, it falls under the genus Zephyranthes, which contains around 80 species of flowering plants. Zephyranthes ischihualasta is native to Mexico and is often found in rocky areas. The classification is further supported by the plant's characteristics, such as its white flowers with yellow centers and strap-like leaves. As a member of the Amaryllidaceae family, Zephyranthes ischihualasta is closely related to other flowering plants like daffodils and lilies.

intro: Zephyranthes ischihualasta, also known as the Mexican rain lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and infections, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes ischihualasta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as gallic acid and quercetin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
